revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''神戸水天宮'''（こうべすいてんぐう）は[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[長田区]]にある神社（[[水天宮]]）。'
revision.text: '''神戸水天宮'''（こうべすいてんぐう）は[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[長田区]]にある神社（[[水天宮]]）。

'''神戸水天宮'''（こうべすいてんぐう）は[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[長田区]]にある神社（[[水天宮]]）。
{{Substub}}

'''神戸水天宮'''（こうべすいてんぐう）は[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[長田区]]にある神社（[[水天宮]]）。 == 祭神 == ・[[天御中主神]]
・高倉平中宮　建礼門院（[[平徳子]]）
・[[安徳天皇]]
・平従二位時子（[[平時子]]） == 由緒 == 社伝によれば、当宮の鎮座する天神山には、『神代の頃に事代主神が天より降りてこられた』との言い伝えがあり、宇奈弖（うなで）の丘と称して神聖な山として守られてきた。[[大正]]三年、当宮の初代宮司 西 学治が中心となって、[[福岡県]][[久留米市]]鎮座の[[水天宮 (久留米市)]]より御分霊を勧請し、[[兵庫区]]楠町に奉安の後、[[長田区]]熊野神社内に奉斎した。[[大正]]十一年十月、この宇奈弖の丘に仮本宮を建立して遷宮。[[大正]]十二年四月、本宮に遷御し、神戸水天宮として鎮祭した。

'''神戸水天宮'''（こうべすいてんぐう）は[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[長田区]]にある神社（[[水天宮]]）。 == 祭神 == ・[[天御中主神]]
・高倉平中宮　建礼門院（[[平徳子]]）
・[[安徳天皇]]
・平従二位時子（[[平時子]]） == 由緒 == 社伝によれば、当宮の鎮座する天神山には、『神代の頃に事代主神が天より降りてこられた』との言い伝えがあり、宇奈弖（うなで）の丘と称して神聖な山として守られてきた。[[大正]]三年、当宮の初代宮司 西 学治が中心となって、[[福岡県]][[久留米市]]鎮座の[[水天宮 (久留米市)]]より御分霊を勧請し、[[兵庫区]]楠町に奉安の後、[[長田区]]熊野神社内に奉斎した。[[大正]]十一年十月、この宇奈弖の丘に仮本宮を建立して遷宮。[[大正]]十二年四月、本宮に遷御し、神戸水天宮として鎮祭した。 == 境内社 == ・長田社(ながたしゃ)　　　祭神：[[事代主]]神
・白長社(しらながしゃ)　　祭神：[[大物主]]神
・種宮(とくさのみや)　　　 祭神：西(にし)家（創始社家）御神霊 == 祭典 == ・春の大祭：　４月５日
・秋の大祭：１０月５日 == 交通 == ・『神鉄長田駅』より徒歩約１０分
・駐車スペース：参道内に有 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.kobesuitenguu.jp/ 神戸水天宮]
*[http://blog.kobesuitenguu.jp/ 神戸水天宮（すいてんぐうさん）のブログ]

・高倉平中宮　建礼門院（[[平徳子]]）
・[[安徳天皇]]
・平従二位時子（[[平時子]]） == 由緒 == 社伝によれば、当宮の鎮座する天神山には、『神代の頃に事代主神が天より降りてこられた』との言い伝えがあり、宇奈弖（うなで）の丘と称して神聖な山として守られてきた。[[大正]]三年、当宮の初代宮司 西 学治が中心となって、[[福岡県]][[久留米市]]鎮座の[[水天宮 (久留米市)]]より御分霊を勧請し、[[兵庫区]]楠町に奉安の後、[[長田区]]熊野神社内に奉斎した。[[大正]]十一年十月、この宇奈弖の丘に仮本宮を建立して遷宮。[[大正]]十二年四月、本宮に遷御し、神戸水天宮として鎮祭した。 == 境内社 == ・長田社(ながたしゃ)　　　祭神：[[事代主]]神
・白長社(しらながしゃ)　　祭神：[[大物主]]神
・種宮(とくさのみや)　　　 祭神：西(にし)家（創始社家）御神霊 == 祭典 == ・春の大祭：　４月５日
・秋の大祭：１０月５日 == 交通 == ・『[[長田駅 (神戸電鉄)]]』より徒歩約１０分
・駐車スペース：参道内に有 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.kobesuitenguu.jp/ 神戸水天宮]
*[http://blog.kobesuitenguu.jp/ 神戸水天宮（すいてんぐうさん）のブログ]

{{神社 |名称 = 神戸水天宮 |画像 = |所在地 = 兵庫県神戸市長田区長田天神町五丁目5-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|55.4|N|135|08|46.3|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 天御中主神 |社格 = |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = 水天宮さん |札所 = |例祭 = 10月5日 |神事 = }}
'''神戸水天宮'''（こうべすいてんぐう）は[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[長田区]]長田天神町に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[水天宮]]）である。
== 祭神 == [[天御中主神]]を主祭神とし、[[安徳天皇]]、[[平徳子|建礼門院（平徳子）]]、[[平時子|二位尼（にいのあま）平時子]]を[[配祀]]する。
== 由緒 == 社伝によれば、当宮の鎮座する天神山には、「[[神代]]の頃に[[事代主神]]が天より降りてこられた」との言い伝えがあり、「宇奈弖（うなで）の丘」と称して神聖な山として守られてきた。[[大正]]3年（[[1914年]]）、当宮の初代[[宮司]]西学治が中心となって、[[福岡県]][[久留米市]]鎮座の[[水天宮 (久留米市)|水天宮]]より[[分霊]]を[[勧請]]し、[[兵庫区]]楠町に奉安の後、[[長田区]]熊野神社内に奉斎した。[[大正]]11年10月、この宇奈弖の丘に仮本宮を建立して遷宮、翌12年4月、本宮に遷御し、「神戸水天宮」として鎮祭した。
== 境内社 == *長田社（ながたしゃ） 祭神：事代主神 *白長社（しらながしゃ） 祭神：[[大物主神]] *種宮（とくさのみや） 祭神：西（にし）家（創始社家）御神霊
== 祭典 == *春の大祭：4月5日 *秋の大祭：10月5日
== 交通 == *[[神戸電鉄]][[長田駅 (神戸電鉄)|長田駅]]より徒歩約10分
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kobesuitenguu.jp/ 神戸水天宮]・[http://blog.kobesuitenguu.jp/ 神戸水天宮（すいてんぐうさん）のブログ]（神社公式） *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301170.html 神戸水天宮]（兵庫県神社庁）
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうへすいてんくう}} [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:水天宮]] [[Category:長田区]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
__NOTOC__ {{Shrine |Name = Kobe Suitengū |Image = [[file:神戸水天宮拝殿.jpg|200px]] |Location = {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}{{ill|Nagata Ward|ja|長田区}}{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町}}五丁目5番1号 |ISO = JP-28 |Latitude° = 34 | Latitude′ = 40 | Latitude″ = 55.4 | N (North) or S (South) = N |Longitude° = 135 | Longitude′ = 08 | Longitude″ = 46.3 | E (East) or W (West) = E |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Shrine = |Also Known As = Suiten-gu-san |Fuda Place = |Annual Festival = October 5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Hyogo }}
'''Kobe Suitengū'''（こうべすいてんぐう） is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Suiten-gu|ja|水天宮}}) located in {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Kobe City|ja|神戸市}}{{ill|Nagata Ward|ja|長田区}}{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町}}.
== Enshrined Deities == It enshrines {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines {{ill|Antoku Emperor|ja|安徳天皇}}, {{ill|Heitokuji|ja|平徳子|lt=Kenreimonin (Heitokuji)|lt_ja=建礼門院（平徳子）}}, and {{ill|Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi|ja|平時子|lt=Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi|lt_ja=二位尼（にいのあま）平時子}}.
== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). In Taishō 3 ({{ill|1914|ja|1914年}}), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ({{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊}}) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}{{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市}} ({{ill|Suiten-gu (Kurume City)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suiten-gu|lt_ja=水天宮}}). After an offering at Kusunoki in {{ill|Hyogo Ward|ja|兵庫区}}, it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in {{ill|Nagata Ward|ja|長田区}}. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Kobe Electric Railway|ja|神戸電鉄}}’s {{ill|Nagata Station|ja|長田駅 (神戸電鉄)|lt=Nagata Station|lt_ja=長田駅}} * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)
== Footnotes == {{Footnote Help}}
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
=== References === <!-- Bibliography reference page --> {{Reflist|2}} <!-- == Bibliography == --> <!-- List of source documents used in this article -->
== External Links == * [http://www.kobesuitenguu.jp/ Kobe Suitengū]・[http://blog.kobesuitenguu.jp/ Kobe Suitengū (Suiten-gu-san) Blog] (Official Shrine) * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301170.html Kobe Suitengū] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Agency)

revision.text:
__NOTOC__ {{神社 |名称 = 神戸水天宮 |画像 = [[file:神戸水天宮拝殿.jpg|200px]] |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[長田区]][[長田天神町]]五丁目5番1号 |ISO =JP-28 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 40 | 緯度秒 = 55.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 08 | 経度秒 = 46.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神 = 天御中主神 |社格 = |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = 水天宮さん |札所 = |例祭 = 10月5日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Hyogo }} '''神戸水天宮'''（こうべすいてんぐう）は[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[長田区]][[長田天神町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]（[[水天宮]]）である。
== 祭神 == [[天御中主神]]を主祭神とし、[[安徳天皇]]、[[平徳子|建礼門院（平徳子）]]、[[平時子|二位尼（にいのあま）平時子]]を[[配祀]]する。
== 由緒 == 社伝によれば、当宮の鎮座する天神山には、「[[神代]]の頃に[[事代主神]]が天より降りてこられた」との言い伝えがあり、「宇奈弖（うなで）の丘」と称して神聖な山として守られてきた。[[大正]]3年（[[1914年]]）、当宮の初代[[宮司]]西学治が中心となって、[[福岡県]][[久留米市]]鎮座の[[水天宮 (久留米市)|水天宮]]より[[分霊]]を[[勧請]]し、[[兵庫区]]楠町に奉安の後、[[長田区]]熊野神社内に奉斎した。[[大正]]11年10月、この宇奈弖の丘に仮本宮を建立して遷宮、翌12年4月、本宮に遷御し、「神戸水天宮」として鎮祭した。
== 境内社 == * 長田社（ながたしゃ） 祭神： [[事代主神]] * 白長社（しらながしゃ） 祭神： [[大物主神]] * 種宮（とくさのみや） 祭神： 西（にし）家（創始社家）御神霊
== 祭典 == * 春の大祭： 4月5日 * 秋の大祭： 10月5日
== 交通 == * [[神戸電鉄]][[長田駅 (神戸電鉄)|長田駅]]より徒歩約10分 * [[神戸市バス]][[名倉小学校]]前下車（2023年12月時点で車内案内放送あり）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
=== 参照 === <!-- 文献参照ページ --> {{Reflist|2}} <!-- == 参考文献 == --> <!-- この項目に使用した出典文献一覧 -->
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kobesuitenguu.jp/ 神戸水天宮]・[http://blog.kobesuitenguu.jp/ 神戸水天宮（すいてんぐうさん）のブログ]（神社公式） * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301170.html 神戸水天宮]（兵庫県神社庁）
{{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうへすいてんくう}} [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:水天宮]] [[Category:長田区の歴史]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} [[Category:長田区の建築物]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kobe Suiten-gū]][[ja:神戸水天宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11590168]] Wikidata name: Kobe Suiten-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11590168]] Wikidata name: Kobe Suiten-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} __NOTOC__ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kobe Suitengū |image =File:神戸水天宮拝殿.jpg |Location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]][[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}五丁目5番1号 |ISO = JP-28 |Latitude° = 34 | Latitude′ = 40 | Latitude″ = 55.4 | N (North) or S (South) = N |Longitude° = 135 | Longitude′ = 08 | Longitude″ = 46.3 | E (East) or W (West) = E |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Shrine = |Also Known As = Suiten-gu-san |Fuda Place = |Annual Festival = October 5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Hyōgo |location = {{ill|Nagata-ku|qid=Q1078801}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.682056|N|135.146194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779}} }}
'''Kobe Suitengū'''（こうべすいてんぐう） is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] ([[:en:Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]) located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]][[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Enshrined Deities == It enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], [[:en:Taira no Tokuko|Kenreimonin (Heitokuji)]], and [[:en:Taira no Tokiko|Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi]].
== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojidai|qq=|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). In Taishō 3 ([[:en:1914|1914]]), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|qq=|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]] ([[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]). After an offering at Kusunoki in [[:en:Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|Hyōgo Ward]], it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in [[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Kobe Electric Railway|Kobe Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|Nagata Station]] * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|qq=|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|qq=|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)
== Footnotes ==
=== Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
=== References ===
{{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kobesuitenguu.jp/ Kobe Suitengū]・[http://blog.kobesuitenguu.jp/ Kobe Suitengū (Suiten-gu-san) Blog] (Official Shrine) * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301170.html Kobe Suitengū] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency)

In Taishō 3 ([[:en:1914|1914]]), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|qq=|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]] ([[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]). '''Kobe Suitengū'''（こうべすいてんぐう） is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] ([[:en:Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]) located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]][[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Enshrined Deities == It enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], [[:en:Taira no Tokuko|Kenreimonin (Heitokuji)]], and [[:en:Taira no Tokiko|Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi]].
== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojidai|qq=|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). In Taishō 3 ([[:en:1914|1914]]), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|qq=|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]] ([[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]). After an offering at Kusunoki in [[:en:Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|Hyōgo Ward]], it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in [[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Kobe Electric Railway|Kobe Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|Nagata Station]] * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|qq=|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|qq=|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)

== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Kobe Electric Railway|Kobe Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|Nagata Station]] * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)

'''Kobe Suitengū'''（こうべすいてんぐう） is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] ([[:en:Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]) located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]][[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Enshrined Deities == It enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], [[:en:Taira no Tokuko|Kenreimonin (Heitokuji)]], and [[:en:Taira no Tokiko|Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi]].
== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojidai|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). In Taishō 3 ([[:en:1914|1914]]), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]] ([[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]). After an offering at Kusunoki in [[:en:Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|Hyōgo Ward]], it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in [[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Kobe Electric Railway|Kobe Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|Nagata Station]] * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)

== Enshrined Deities == It enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], [[:en:Taira no Tokuko|Kenreimonin (Heitokuji)]], and [[:en:Taira no Tokiko|Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi]].
== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojidai|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). In Taishō 3 ([[:en:1914|1914]]), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]] ([[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]). After an offering at Kusunoki in [[:en:Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|Hyōgo Ward]], it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in [[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Kobe Electric Railway|Kobe Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|Nagata Station]] * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)

== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Kobe Electric Railway|Kobe Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|Nagata Station]] * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)

'''Kobe Suitengū'''（こうべすいてんぐう） is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] ([[:en:Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]) located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]][[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojidai|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). After an offering at Kusunoki in [[:en:Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|Hyōgo Ward]], it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in [[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kobe Suiten-gū]][[ja:神戸水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kobe Suiten-gū]][[ja:神戸水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kobe Suiten-gū]][[ja:神戸水天宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11590168]] Wikidata name: Kobe Suiten-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11590168]] Wikidata name: Kobe Suiten-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} __NOTOC__ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kobe Suitengū |image =File:神戸水天宮拝殿.jpg |Location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]][[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}五丁目5番1号 |ISO = JP-28 |Latitude° = 34 | Latitude′ = 40 | Latitude″ = 55.4 | N (North) or S (South) = N |Longitude° = 135 | Longitude′ = 08 | Longitude″ = 46.3 | E (East) or W (West) = E |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Shrine = |Also Known As = Suiten-gu-san |Fuda Place = |Annual Festival = October 5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Hyōgo |location = {{ill|Nagata-ku|WD=Q1078801}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.682056|N|135.146194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} }}
'''Kobe Suitengū'''（こうべすいてんぐう） is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] ([[:en:Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]) located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Kobe|Kobe City]][[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Enshrined Deities == It enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]], [[:en:Taira no Tokuko|Kenreimonin (Heitokuji)]], and [[:en:Taira no Tokiko|Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi]].
== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojidai|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). In Taishō 3 ([[:en:1914|1914]]), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]] ([[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]). After an offering at Kusunoki in [[:en:Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|Hyōgo Ward]], it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in [[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Kobe Electric Railway|Kobe Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|Nagata Station]] * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
=== References ===
{{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kobesuitenguu.jp/ Kobe Suitengū]・[http://blog.kobesuitenguu.jp/ Kobe Suitengū (Suiten-gu-san) Blog] (Official Shrine) * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301170.html Kobe Suitengū] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency)

In Taishō 3 ([[:en:1914|1914]]), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ([[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei]]) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]] ([[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suiten-gu]]). After an offering at Kusunoki in [[:en:Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|Hyōgo Ward]], it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in [[:en:Nagata-ku, Kobe|Nagata Ward]]. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10‑minute walk from [[:en:Kobe Electric Railway|Kobe Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|Nagata Station]] * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kobe Suiten-gū]][[ja:神戸水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kobe Suiten-gū]][[ja:神戸水天宮]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kobe Suiten-gū]][[ja:神戸水天宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11590168]] Wikidata name: Kobe Suiten-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11590168]] Wikidata name: Kobe Suiten-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} __NOTOC__ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kobe Suitengū |image =File:神戸水天宮拝殿.jpg |Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}{{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward}}{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}五丁目5番1号 |ISO = JP-28 |Latitude° = 34 | Latitude′ = 40 | Latitude″ = 55.4 | N (North) or S (South) = N |Longitude° = 135 | Longitude′ = 08 | Longitude″ = 46.3 | E (East) or W (West) = E |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Shrine = |Also Known As = Suiten-gu-san |Fuda Place = |Annual Festival = October 5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Hyōgo |location = {{ill|Nagata-ku|WD=Q1078801}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.682056|N|135.146194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} }}
'''Kobe Suitengū'''（こうべすいてんぐう） is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} ({{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suiten-gu}}) located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}{{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward}}{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Enshrined Deities == It enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}}, {{ill|Taira no Tokuko|en|Taira no Tokuko|WD=Q3315855|lt=Kenreimonin (Heitokuji)}}, and {{ill|Taira no Tokiko|en|Taira no Tokiko|WD=Q3379244|lt=Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi}}.
== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojidai|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). In Taishō 3 ({{ill|1914|en|1914|WD=Q2083|lt=1914}}), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei}}) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume City}} ({{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Suiten-gu}}). After an offering at Kusunoki in {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward}}, it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in {{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward}}. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Kobe Electric Railway|en|Kobe Electric Railway|WD=Q836658|lt=Kobe Electric Railway}}’s {{ill|Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|en|Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|WD=Q6958663|lt=Nagata Station}} * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
=== References ===
{{Reflist|2}}

__NOTOC__ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kobe Suitengū |image =File:神戸水天宮拝殿.jpg |Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}{{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward}}{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}五丁目5番1号 |ISO = JP-28 |Latitude° = 34 | Latitude′ = 40 | Latitude″ = 55.4 | N (North) or S (South) = N |Longitude° = 135 | Longitude′ = 08 | Longitude″ = 46.3 | E (East) or W (West) = E |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Shrine = |Also Known As = Suiten-gu-san |Fuda Place = |Annual Festival = October 5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Hyōgo |location = {{ill|Nagata-ku|WD=Q1078801}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.682056|N|135.146194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} }}
'''Kobe Suitengū'''（こうべすいてんぐう） is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} ({{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suiten-gu}}) located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City}}{{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward}}{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Enshrined Deities == It enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}}, {{ill|Taira no Tokuko|en|Taira no Tokuko|WD=Q3315855|lt=Kenreimonin (Heitokuji)}}, and {{ill|Taira no Tokiko|en|Taira no Tokiko|WD=Q3379244|lt=Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi}}.
== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojidai|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). In Taishō 3 ({{ill|1914|en|1914|WD=Q2083|lt=1914}}), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei}}) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume City}} ({{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Suiten-gu}}). After an offering at Kusunoki in {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward}}, it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in {{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward}}. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10‑minute walk from {{ill|Kobe Electric Railway|en|Kobe Electric Railway|WD=Q836658|lt=Kobe Electric Railway}}’s {{ill|Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|en|Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|WD=Q6958663|lt=Nagata Station}} * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)
== Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Notes"}}
=== References ===
{{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kobesuitenguu.jp/ Kobe Suitengū]・[http://blog.kobesuitenguu.jp/ Kobe Suitengū (Suiten-gu-san) Blog] (Official Shrine) * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301170.html Kobe Suitengū] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Agency)

'''Kobe Suitengū'''（こうべすいてんぐう） is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suiten-gu|1=Suitengū}}) located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}{{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward|1=Nagata-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Enshrined Deities == It enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}, {{ill|Taira no Tokuko|en|Taira no Tokuko|WD=Q3315855|lt=Kenreimonin (Heitokuji)|1=Taira no Tokuko}}, and {{ill|Taira no Tokiko|en|Taira no Tokiko|WD=Q3379244|lt=Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi|1=Taira no Tokiko}}.
== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojidai|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). In Taishō 3 ({{ill|1914|en|1914|WD=Q2083|lt=1914|1=1914}}), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}}) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume City|1=Kurume}} ({{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Suiten-gu|1=Kurume Suitengū}}). After an offering at Kusunoki in {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}, it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in {{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward|1=Nagata-ku, Kobe}}. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kobe Electric Railway|en|Kobe Electric Railway|WD=Q836658|lt=Kobe Electric Railway|1=Kobe Electric Railway}}’s {{ill|Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|en|Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|WD=Q6958663|lt=Nagata Station|1=Nagata Station (Shintetsu)}} * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)

__NOTOC__ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kobe Suitengū |image =File:神戸水天宮拝殿.jpg |Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}{{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward|1=Nagata-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}五丁目5番1号 |ISO = JP-28 |Latitude° = 34 | Latitude′ = 40 | Latitude″ = 55.4 | N (North) or S (South) = N |Longitude° = 135 | Longitude′ = 08 | Longitude″ = 46.3 | E (East) or W (West) = E |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Shrine = |Also Known As = Suiten-gu-san |Fuda Place = |Annual Festival = October 5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Hyōgo |location = {{ill|Nagata-ku|WD=Q1078801|1=Nagata-ku, Kobe}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.682056|N|135.146194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} }}
'''Kobe Suitengū'''（こうべすいてんぐう） is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|WD=Q125319456|lt=Suiten-gu|1=Suitengū}}) located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}{{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward|1=Nagata-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Enshrined Deities == It enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}, {{ill|Taira no Tokuko|en|Taira no Tokuko|WD=Q3315855|lt=Kenreimonin (Heitokuji)|1=Taira no Tokuko}}, and {{ill|Taira no Tokiko|en|Taira no Tokiko|WD=Q3379244|lt=Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi|1=Taira no Tokiko}}.
== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojidai|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). In Taishō 3 ({{ill|1914|en|1914|WD=Q2083|lt=1914|1=1914}}), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}}) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume City|1=Kurume}} ({{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|WD=Q3200625|lt=Suiten-gu|1=Kurume Suitengū}}). After an offering at Kusunoki in {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}, it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in {{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|WD=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward|1=Nagata-ku, Kobe}}. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kobe Electric Railway|en|Kobe Electric Railway|WD=Q836658|lt=Kobe Electric Railway|1=Kobe Electric Railway}}’s {{ill|Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|en|Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|WD=Q6958663|lt=Nagata Station|1=Nagata Station (Shintetsu)}} * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)

__NOTOC__ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Kobe Suitengū |image =File:神戸水天宮拝殿.jpg |Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}{{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward|1=Nagata-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}五丁目5番1号 |ISO = JP-28 |Latitude° = 34 | Latitude′ = 40 | Latitude″ = 55.4 | N (North) or S (South) = N |Longitude° = 135 | Longitude′ = 08 | Longitude″ = 46.3 | E (East) or W (West) = E |Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Shrine = |Also Known As = Suiten-gu-san |Fuda Place = |Annual Festival = October 5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Hyōgo |location = {{ill|Nagata-ku|qid=Q1078801|1=Nagata-ku, Kobe}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.682056|N|135.146194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} }}
'''Kobe Suitengū'''（こうべすいてんぐう） is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Suitengū|en|Suitengū|qid=Q125319456|lt=Suiten-gu|1=Suitengū}}) located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe City|1=Kobe}}{{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward|1=Nagata-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|Nagata Tenjin Town|ja|長田天神町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagata Tenjin Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Enshrined Deities == It enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} as its principal deity, and also enshrines {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|qid=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}, {{ill|Taira no Tokuko|en|Taira no Tokuko|qid=Q3315855|lt=Kenreimonin (Heitokuji)|1=Taira no Tokuko}}, and {{ill|Taira no Tokiko|en|Taira no Tokiko|qid=Q3379244|lt=Second-Rank Nun Hira Tokushi|1=Taira no Tokiko}}.
== History == According to the shrine legend, on Mount Tenjin—the location of this shrine—there is a tradition that during the time of {{ill|Kojidai|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojidai|1=Age of the gods (Japanese mythology)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3577055}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} descended from heaven, and the mountain was revered as a sacred mountain called Unade Hill (宇奈弖（うなで）の丘). In Taishō 3 ({{ill|1914|en|1914|qid=Q2083|lt=1914|1=1914}}), the shrine’s first {{ill|Chief Priest|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Priest|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453500}}, Nishigakuji, took the initiative to enshrine a branch spirit ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|qid=Q195793|lt=Bunrei|1=Bunrei}}) from the Suiten-gu enshrined in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|qid=Q320934|lt=Kurume City|1=Kurume}} ({{ill|Kurume Suitengū|en|Kurume Suitengū|qid=Q3200625|lt=Suiten-gu|1=Kurume Suitengū}}). After an offering at Kusunoki in {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}, it was temporarily enshrined at Kumano Shrine in {{ill|Nagata-ku, Kobe|en|Nagata-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1078801|lt=Nagata Ward|1=Nagata-ku, Kobe}}. In Taishō 11, October, a provisional main shrine was established on Unade Hill; and in the following April of Taishō 12, the shrine was transferred to its main location and enshrined as “Kobe Suitengū.”
== Sub-shrines == * Nagata Sha (ながたしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * Shirananaga Sha (しらながしゃ) Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * Tokuza-no-miya (とくさのみや) Deity: The sacred spirit of the founding Nishi family
== Festivals == * Spring Grand Festival: April 5 * Autumn Grand Festival: October 5
== Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kobe Electric Railway|en|Kobe Electric Railway|qid=Q836658|lt=Kobe Electric Railway|1=Kobe Electric Railway}}’s {{ill|Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|en|Nagata Station (Shintetsu)|qid=Q6958663|lt=Nagata Station|1=Nagata Station (Shintetsu)}} * Alight at in front of {{ill|Nagura Elementary School|ja|名倉小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagura Elementary School|1=Nagura Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105351933}} via {{ill|Kobe City Bus|ja|神戸市バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Bus|1=Kobe City Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11589912}} (as of December 2023, there is an onboard announcement)
